metadata.title: AI Data Center Supply Shortage Tracker
metadata.subtitle: End-to-End Component & Supplier Intelligence
metadata.update_note: Data sourced from latest earnings reports and supply chain intelligence as of March 2026

tier_legend.T1: Primary Components — the actual products procured and deployed in data centers
tier_legend.T2: Sub-Components & Supply Chain Inputs — what goes inside T1 components; tracked for upstream risk

components.name: GPU Boards
components.headline: Critical — Blackwell sold out through mid-2026; HBM3E & CoWoS bottlenecks persist into 2028; CPUs tightening

subcomponents.name: NVIDIA Blackwell B200 / B100 GPUs
subcomponents.shortage_summary: Sold out through mid-2026 with a 3.6M unit backlog; constrained by CoWoS packaging and HBM3E allocation.

subcomponents.name: NVIDIA H200 / H100 GPUs (Hopper)
subcomponents.shortage_summary: Supply improving as Blackwell ramps, but sustained demand from customers unable to access Blackwell keeps lead times elevated.

subcomponents.name: AMD MI300X / MI325X GPUs
subcomponents.shortage_summary: Supply broadly adequate; MI300X uses HBM3 (not HBM3E) and faces less CoWoS pressure than NVIDIA — ROCm adoption the bigger constraint.

subcomponents.name: Intel Xeon CPUs (Sapphire / Emerald Rapids)
subcomponents.shortage_summary: High-core-count SKUs (60-core+) constrained for AI server builds; lead times extended to 16–20 weeks as AI server demand accelerates.

subcomponents.name: AMD EPYC CPUs (Genoa / Turin)
subcomponents.shortage_summary: Turin 128-core SKUs face supply tightness as TSMC N4 wafer allocation prioritizes GPU production; lead times at 14–18 weeks.

subcomponents.name: CoWoS Advanced Packaging (TSMC)
subcomponents.shortage_summary: Running 20% below 2026 demand at 75K wafers/month; scaling to 130K by late 2026, but demand growth means consistent sufficiency not until H2 2027.

subcomponents.name: HBM3E Memory (SK Hynix / Micron / Samsung)
subcomponents.shortage_summary: All suppliers fully contracted through 2026 and much of 2027; new HBM fab capacity takes 2+ years — analyst consensus is consistent sufficiency in 2028.

subcomponents.name: TSMC 3nm / 5nm Wafer Fabrication
subcomponents.shortage_summary: N3/N4/N5 fully allocated; TSMC Arizona N4 ramping but insufficient to relieve Taiwan — consistent sufficiency tied to N2 node ramp in 2027–2028.

subcomponents.name: Advanced Server PCBs & Substrates
subcomponents.shortage_summary: High-layer-count GPU board substrates at 16–20 week lead times; TTM Technologies and Unimicron adding capacity through mid-2026.

supply_chain.notes: NVIDIA holds >80% AI GPU market share
N3/N4/N5 fully allocated; Arizona ramp not yet material
All 2026 production contracted; consistent supply not until 2028
Binding bottleneck — 20% shortfall vs. demand; H2 2027 relief
All ODMs fully booked through 2026
Final rack-level integration constrained by GPU supply

suppliers.name: NVIDIA
suppliers.growth_reason: Blackwell ramp + reasoning AI creating second demand wave on top of training; data center now >90% of revenue

suppliers.name: TSMC
suppliers.growth_reason: AI chip surge driving N3/N4 revenue; CoWoS sold out; capex raised to record $52–56B for 2026 expansion

suppliers.name: AMD
suppliers.growth_reason: MI300X/MI325X hyperscaler traction + EPYC Turin fastest-ramping CPU in AMD history; data center ~50% of revenue

suppliers.name: SK Hynix
suppliers.growth_reason: HBM revenue doubled YoY; holds ~70% of NVIDIA's HBM4 allocation; all 2026 and much of 2027 production already contracted

suppliers.name: Micron Technology
suppliers.growth_reason: HBM Q4 revenue near $2B; 100% of 2026 HBM contracted; data center DRAM now 56% of sales at premium pricing

components.name: Memory & Storage
components.headline: Watch List — High-density DDR5 RDIMMs constrained through 2028 as fabs prioritize HBM; NVMe & CXL supply adequate; HBM itself tracked within GPU Boards

subcomponents.name: DDR5 Server RDIMMs (256GB / 512GB High-Density)
subcomponents.shortage_summary: High-density server DIMMs constrained as Samsung, SK Hynix, and Micron divert DRAM fab capacity to HBM3E, which commands 5–8× the ASP per die. 256GB+ modules at 16–20 week lead times.

subcomponents.name: Enterprise NVMe SSDs (U.2 / E3.S, 15–30TB)
subcomponents.shortage_summary: NAND flash in global oversupply; enterprise SSDs broadly available. AI training datasets and KV-cache storage are driving strong demand but supply is keeping pace comfortably.

subcomponents.name: CXL Memory Modules (CXL 2.0 / 3.0)
subcomponents.shortage_summary: Supply building ahead of demand; hyperscaler CXL deployments still nascent in 2026. Micron CZ120 and Samsung CMM modules available but not yet in volume production at scale.

subcomponents.name: DDR5 DRAM Die (High-Bandwidth Server Configurations)
subcomponents.shortage_summary: All three major DRAM makers (Samsung, SK Hynix, Micron) prioritizing HBM3E over DDR5 due to dramatically higher margins. Constrains high-density DIMM supply through 2027.

subcomponents.name: NAND Flash (TLC / QLC Enterprise Grade)
subcomponents.shortage_summary: Structural oversupply through 2026; Samsung, Kioxia, and Micron running elevated inventory. Prices recovering from 2024 trough but supply is not a binding constraint.

supply_chain.notes: All three prioritizing HBM3E over DDR5; high-density DIMM supply constrained

supply_chain.player: Samsung / Kioxia / Micron / SK Hynix / WD
supply_chain.notes: Global oversupply; prices recovering from 2024 trough — not a bottleneck

supply_chain.player: Samsung / Kioxia / Micron / WD / SK Hynix
supply_chain.notes: Vertically integrated; supply tracks NAND availability — adequate
CXL 2.0/3.0 memory pooling; supply ahead of hyperscaler adoption curve
Vertically integrated memory makers; bottleneck is die allocation, not assembly

suppliers.name: Micron Technology
suppliers.growth_reason: Data center DRAM (DDR5 + HBM) now 56% of revenue; CXL memory modules entering hyperscaler qualification; $20B capex expanding Boise and Singapore fabs

suppliers.name: Samsung Electronics
suppliers.growth_reason: HBM3 ramp lagging SK Hynix; legacy DRAM/NAND oversupply pressuring margins; investing heavily in HBM4 and advanced packaging to close competitive gap

suppliers.name: SK Hynix
suppliers.role: DDR5 RDIMMs & NAND (HBM tracked in GPU Boards)
suppliers.growth_reason: HBM dominance lifting all memory margins; DDR5 server DRAM benefiting from AI server build-out; NAND recovering from 2024 oversupply trough

suppliers.name: Kioxia
suppliers.growth_reason: NAND recovery from 2024 trough; AI storage demand (training datasets, KV cache) driving enterprise SSD growth; Yokkaichi and Kitakami fabs ramping BiCS8

suppliers.name: Western Digital
suppliers.growth_reason: Flash segment driven by AI enterprise SSD demand; cloud revenue +60% YoY; Ultrastar DC SN840 seeing strong data center adoption

components.name: Scale-Out Networking
components.headline: Moderate — EML laser is the critical sub-component; Ethernet switches ramping; InfiniBand supply easing

subcomponents.name: Ethernet Switches (400G / 800G)
subcomponents.shortage_summary: Tomahawk 6-based systems entering volume Q2 2026 but Broadcom's $73B backlog means consistent supply not before H1 2027.

subcomponents.name: InfiniBand Switches & NICs (NDR 400G)
subcomponents.shortage_summary: 2024–2025 silicon constraints resolved; market also shifting toward Ethernet for new builds, moderating InfiniBand demand.

subcomponents.name: Optical Transceivers (800G / 1.6T)
subcomponents.shortage_summary: NVIDIA pre-allocated Lumentum's EML laser capacity through 2027; InP fab expansion takes 2–3 years — consistent sufficiency 2028.

subcomponents.name: Smart NICs / DPUs (ConnectX-8, BlueField-3)
subcomponents.shortage_summary: NVIDIA NIC production competing for TSMC allocation vs. GPU priority; lead times at 20–26 weeks.

subcomponents.name: Ethernet Switch ASICs (Tomahawk 6 / Teralynx 10)
subcomponents.shortage_summary: Broadcom Tomahawk 6 (102.4 Tb/s) in early ramp; both ASICs competing for TSMC N5/N6 alongside GPU production.

subcomponents.name: EML Laser Dies (200G-per-lane InP)
subcomponents.shortage_summary: Lumentum is sole volume supplier; NVIDIA pre-allocated capacity through 2027 — other buyers face 25–30% supply gaps until new InP fabs come online.

subcomponents.name: Silicon Photonics Chips (EML Alternative)
subcomponents.shortage_summary: Growing as EML alternative for 800G/1.6T; Intel, Broadcom and Marvell all expanding SiPh capacity through 2026.

supply_chain.notes: Broadcom ~90% cloud DC switch ASIC market share
N5/N6 — competing with GPU production for capacity
Critical bottleneck — NVIDIA pre-allocated Lumentum through 2027
Fabrinet key contract manufacturer; Coherent vertically integrated
Tomahawk 6 / Teralynx 10 designs entering volume Q2 2026
NVIDIA ConnectX-8 dominant in AI cluster NIC market

suppliers.name: Broadcom
suppliers.growth_reason: Tomahawk 6 ramp + custom AI chips (Google TPU, Meta MTIA); AI segment >40% of revenue; $73B AI backlog

suppliers.name: Marvell Technology
suppliers.growth_reason: Custom AI silicon for Google/Amazon + Teralynx 10 Ethernet ASICs; electro-optics products at record revenue

suppliers.name: Lumentum Holdings
suppliers.growth_reason: Sole volume supplier of 200G EML lasers; NVIDIA pre-allocation through 2027 locks in revenue visibility

suppliers.name: Coherent Corp
suppliers.growth_reason: 800G and 1.6T adoption accelerating simultaneously; vertically integrated InP laser supply is a key competitive advantage

suppliers.name: Fabrinet
suppliers.growth_reason: Fastest YoY growth since IPO; Building 10 (2M sq ft) adding 250K sq ft by June 2026; HPC division accelerating

components.name: Scale-Up Networking
components.headline: Moderate — NVSwitch/NVLink constrained as part of Blackwell system; PCIe Gen 6 retimers tight

subcomponents.name: NVSwitch 5 Systems (GPU-to-GPU in rack)
subcomponents.shortage_summary: Constrained as part of the NVL72 Blackwell rack — not a standalone bottleneck, but availability tied to the full GPU system backlog.

subcomponents.name: NVLink 5 Cables & Connectors
subcomponents.shortage_summary: Passive copper assemblies from multiple qualified vendors (Amphenol, TE, Luxshare) — supply tracks GPU system availability.

subcomponents.name: PCIe Gen 5 / Gen 6 Host Bus Adapters
subcomponents.shortage_summary: Gen 6 HBAs entering volume in 2026; supply chain early-ramp with moderate tightness for composable GPU architecture deployments.

subcomponents.name: AMD Infinity Fabric (MI300X Scale-Up)
subcomponents.shortage_summary: Integrated on-package interconnect — no separate supply risk beyond MI300X GPU availability itself.

subcomponents.name: NVSwitch ASIC (TSMC N4)
subcomponents.shortage_summary: Fabricated at TSMC N4 alongside GPU dies — derivative shortage of overall GPU fab constraints.

subcomponents.name: PCIe Retimers & CXL Controllers
subcomponents.shortage_summary: Astera Labs sole-sourced on key hyperscaler PCIe Gen 6 retimer designs; CXL memory expansion adding to load.

subcomponents.name: High-Speed Copper DAC Cables
subcomponents.shortage_summary: Multiple qualified cable vendors; supply adequate and scales with GPU system shipments.

supply_chain.notes: NVSwitch 5 — 1.8 TB/sec per port; proprietary NVIDIA design
N4 node — same constraints as GPU die fab
Passive copper — adequate supply from multiple vendors
Gen 5/6 retimers and switch fabric
CXL 2.0/3.0 memory pooling growing in scale-up configs

suppliers.name: NVIDIA
suppliers.growth_reason: NVL72 requires 2× NVSwitch per rack — NVSwitch revenue scales directly with Blackwell GPU shipment growth

suppliers.name: Astera Labs
suppliers.growth_reason: Sole-sourced on several hyperscaler PCIe Gen 6 retimer platforms; CXL memory expansion adding new revenue stream

components.name: Cooling
components.headline: Moderate-to-Critical — Cold plates and specialty coolants most constrained; CDU supply improving with aggressive capacity builds

subcomponents.name: CDUs (Coolant Distribution Units)
subcomponents.shortage_summary: Demand surged 156% YoY; Vertiv's 45× CDU capacity expansion and new nVent/Schneider facilities expected to normalize supply by Q4 2026.

subcomponents.name: Cold Plates (Direct GPU Liquid Cooling)
subcomponents.shortage_summary: GPU-generation-specific designs require re-qualification per SKU; precision CNC machining and liquid metal TIM availability are limiting production.

subcomponents.name: Rear-Door Heat Exchangers (RDHx)
subcomponents.shortage_summary: Used for air-to-liquid transition in legacy facilities; stainless steel HX fabrication capacity constrained but expanding.

subcomponents.name: Immersion Cooling Systems
subcomponents.shortage_summary: Tank fabrication and facility integration complexity constrained; two-phase systems impacted by 3M Novec PFAS phase-out.

subcomponents.name: Precision Air Cooling (CRAC / CRAH)
subcomponents.shortage_summary: Adequate for lower-density zones; limited applicability for high-density AI racks exceeding 20 kW.

subcomponents.name: Specialty Coolants & Dielectric Fluids
subcomponents.shortage_summary: 3M Novec PFAS exit (completed 2025) eliminated the leading immersion fluid; alternatives from Chemours, Solvay, and Engineered Fluids scaling slowly.

subcomponents.name: Thermal Interface Materials (Liquid Metal TIM)
subcomponents.shortage_summary: Indium-gallium alloy TIMs for cold plate attachment constrained by global indium supply (competed by InP laser production).

subcomponents.name: Pumps, Valves & Heat Exchangers (CDU sub-components)
subcomponents.shortage_summary: Grundfos and KSB industrial pumps at 16–24 week lead times; global demand surge from data centers, EVs, and semiconductor fabs competing simultaneously.

subcomponents.name: Chillers (Building-Level Cooling Plant)
subcomponents.shortage_summary: Large centrifugal chillers at 18–26 week lead times from Carrier, Trane, York due to compressor sub-component availability.

supply_chain.notes: GPU-gen specific; CNC machining capacity constrained
45× Vertiv expansion in 2024; nVent new MN facility Q1 2026
Scaling for AI demand; 2-phase disrupted by Novec exit
Post-3M PFAS exit supply gap; alternatives scaling
18–26 week lead times; compressor sub-supply constrained
16–24 week lead times; industrial-wide demand surge

suppliers.name: Vertiv Holdings
suppliers.growth_reason: Dominant CDU supplier; 45× CDU capacity expansion delivering; $9.5B backlog at 1.4× book-to-bill; AI now >60% of revenue

suppliers.name: nVent Electric
suppliers.growth_reason: DC orders +65% organically; liquid cooling backlog 3× YoY; NVIDIA partner network; Blaine MN facility doubling footprint

suppliers.name: Schneider Electric
suppliers.growth_reason: Data center cooling is largest growth driver; North America Energy Management +17% YoY; liquid cooling RDHx/CDU orders surging

suppliers.name: Modine Manufacturing
suppliers.growth_reason: DC cooling +20% organic; hyperscaler wins via Airedale brand; $100M new liquid cooling manufacturing investment targeting Q3 2026 ops
